〜は〜ではありません.commonMistakes: Don't shorten to じゃない in formal writing — ではありません is the correct formal negative.

〜さん.commonMistakes: Never use さん with your own name — it sounds arrogant. Use it only when referring to or addressing others.

〜歳（〜さい）.commonMistakes: Use Arabic numerals with さい in everyday writing (20歳), not kanji numerals. Also, don't use さい when asking age — use おいくつ or 何歳 instead.
exampleTrans: My younger sister is 10 years old.

〜は〜です.usage_b: Used to state that A equals or is B
〜は〜です.commonMistakes: Learners often confuse は (topic marker) with が (subject marker). Use は when introducing or re-stating a topic.

〜は〜ですか.commonMistakes: Don't add a question mark (?) in written Japanese — か already signals a question. Use a period (。) at the end.

〜も.usage_a: Replaces は, が, or を
〜も.commonMistakes: も replaces は, が, or を entirely — don't stack them (e.g., はも is wrong). Use も alone in place of the particle.

〜の.commonMistakes: Word order is reversed from English: 日本の食べ物 = 'food of Japan', not 'Japan of food'. The owner always comes before の.

〜人（〜じん）.commonMistakes: Don't confuse じん (nationality) with にん (counter for people). 日本人 = a Japanese person, 三人 = three people.

おいくつ.commonMistakes: おいくつ is very polite — use 何歳ですか for neutral or casual contexts. Avoid directly asking elders 何歳 as it can sound rude.

こちら／そちら／あちら／どちら.usage_b: Used for people, direction, or place
こちら／そちら／あちら／どちら.commonMistakes: どちら can mean both 'which direction' and 'which one (of two)' — context determines the meaning. For three or more choices, use どれ instead.
